steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined portion follows "thus" and describes Duarte's approach: capitalizing on research potential from a vast but underutilized source. The question asks what function this sentence serves in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It specifies potential applications of the approach that Duarte and colleagues used in their study.’: The passage mentions research potential, not specific applications of the technique. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It offers commentary on the significance of the approach that Duarte and colleagues used for their study.’: The phrase "capitalizing on the research potential" is evaluative commentary on the approach itself — it assesses what makes the method noteworthy. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It emphasizes the importance of Duarte and colleagues' findings about the DNA of flora.’: The passage only mentions potential, not findings or discoveries. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It explains why the research methodology selected by Duarte and colleagues is not widely used.’: The passage notes the underutilization as a fact; it does not explain the cause. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
